qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of piping the output of `tar -tvf results.tar` to `wc -l`?
qa_pairs.answer: Piping the output of `tar -tvf results.tar` to `wc -l` is used to count the total number of entries (files and sub-directories) within the specified tar file, `results.tar`.

qa_pairs.question: When using `tar -tvf results.tar | wc -l`, why are the details of the files not displayed, even with the `-v` option?
qa_pairs.answer: The details of the files are not shown because the output from the `tar -tvf` command is filtered through `wc -l`. The `wc -l` command is designed to display only the count of lines, effectively suppressing any detailed file information.
